personal.name: Chris Heher
personal.title: Copywriter | Content Strategist | Conversational Designer
personal.mission: Your customers deserve better than to read robot. They want a human voice behind your brand — one that piques curiosity, not parrots information; one that intrigues, not inundates; and one that doesn't regurgitate words already written, but reveals fresh insights unique to your brand.

philosophy.title: Content isn't read. It's scanned.
philosophy.body: Content is still being written as if it's being read on a page — despite the fact that it's scanned on a screen. I help brands think outside this paper perspective by designing content in ways that reflect the medium it's being consumed on.

philosophy.title: Content written at eye level.
philosophy.body: I take pride in articulating technical concepts in ways that rise above their own jargon. My deep curiosity about a product allows me to write at an audience's eye level — neither talking down to them with oversimplified language nor wallowing in technical lingo that flies over their heads.

philosophy.title: I am a chef. AI is my microwave.
philosophy.body: Content is what brands feed customers to create hunger for their product or service. I've handled all aspects of content creation: setting the menu (content strategy), cooking the meal (copywriting), plating (UX), and even as the maître d' (social media). And like a microwave, AI is an appliance meant to reheat leftovers — not cook a raw meal.

philosophy.title: My process to word processing.
philosophy.body: I am a lifelong timeboxer — I order my productivity in 25-minute increments. Having this reliable process gives clients and colleagues peace of mind that I'll do what I say I can do, and before the deadline I said I'd do it.

projects.title: Cincoro Tequila | brand launch
projects.description: Celebrity tequila brand launch — social media strategy and brand voice for Michael Jordan's tequila company
projects.campaignListDescription: George Clooney. The Rock. Kylie Jenner. Bryan Cranston. Celebrity tequila is a crowded space. So when Michael Jordan and four other NBA owners started their own tequila company, I was there to help them stand out among the stars. Liquor brands are chosen by the stories they tell. Cincoro's story is the competitive spirit shared among its five owners — Cincoro translates to 'five rings.' That spirit distinguishes the brand: it wasn't enough for these owners to play the celebrity tequila game, they had to change it. Working with The Charles advertising agency, we communicated that competitive spirit through website, social, and a foundational manifesto built for the brand's future.
impact: Created content processes and calendar delivering 2–3x higher engagement rates due to consistent posting schedules
Built social audience to 110K+ followers across platforms, exceeding brands with decades of history (Don Julio had ~97K Instagram in 2021)
Brand grew from zero to 1.5M bottles sold

projects.title: Sentry Performance GTM campaign
projects.campaignListDescription: Product releases run the risk of getting bogged down in their own technical benefits. For Sentry's Performance monitoring release, this meant turning improved visibility into p95 response times into a simple, differentiating insight that articulates Sentry's competitive advantage. The result — See Slow Faster — helped Sentry create market share, own a POV as a brand, and build a foundation for future campaigns. Built in close cross-functional collaboration with Sentry's distributed engineering team including the Vienna, Austria office, where I embedded with engineers to understand the product deeply enough to write about it credibly.
impact: $1.8M in attributed sales pipeline
~1.2k content-sourced leads
Campaign content generated ~3.5k visits (2.3x site average)
$1B unicorn valuation achieved months after Performance product launch
Built cross-timezone editorial workflow coordinating US + Vienna (Austria) engineering teams

projects.title: DroneDeploy Safety AI
projects.campaignListDescription: Enterprise-grade drone technology has blockers working against it: concerns over human safety and data privacy, executive resistance toward adopting new technologies, and the widespread perception that a drone is just some neat plaything. As lead writer at DroneDeploy, my role was to think through these blockers and articulate the end benefit that integrated drone software provides teams at worksites — improved collaboration, inch-perfect measurements, and a better way of tracking progress. Not having much of a construction background, I compensated with professional ignorance: deep curiosity combined with close collaboration with solutions engineers and subject matter experts to spin up compelling use cases.
impact: $450K per quarter in attributed content influence (~10% of total pipeline)
~140K views across content assets
20% click-to-open rate for email campaigns
~3K total landing page visits across top-of-funnel content

projects.title: Sentry Dogfooding Chronicles
projects.campaignListDescription: Dogfooding is the process in which a company uses its own product as a quality control mechanism — think: the Hair Club for Men guy. With Dogfooding Chronicles, we could tell the stories of software engineers who used the platform to improve the platform, syndicating unique anecdotes of how dev teams approached the inevitable flaws in their work with honesty and humor. In co-writing these pieces with subject matter experts, I was the producer and the SME was the musician: amplifying insights, syncing strategic points, and modulating their tone.
impact: 25+ blog posts generated ~45K site visits — 40% of which were organic
15+ backlinks per post across key developer publications
~$100K quarterly ARR attributed directly to content series
$100K–$150K annual SEO value (replacement cost of paid search)

links.name: Using Sentry Performance to get Sentry Performant

projects.title: HP Presence
projects.campaignListDescription: The pandemic altered the fabric of society. Nowhere was this more evident than the corporate office. As content strategist on Transmission's HP account, I helped position their Presence videoconferencing suite to be the connective tissue for distributed workforces. The question driving the work: how can businesses build out hybrid infrastructure that seamlessly incorporates devices, solutions, and services across distributed teams — such that remote workers' points can be heard with the same volume and clarity as those physically in the room?
impact: 8,940 total white paper downloads across all 10 assets in launch quarter
~2.5K MQLs generated from white paper download forms
$680K in closed-won deals with white paper download in buyer journey — direct revenue attribution
White paper landing pages drove 34,200 total site visits — 67% of which were organic

links.name: The new office for the way people want to work
A new blueprint for an uncertain world
Get ready today to do tomorrow's work

projects.title: Airbnb Career website
projects.campaignListDescription: Coming out of the pandemic and the subsequent Great Resignation, competition for talent ratcheted up. Companies began treating career websites not as a job repository but as a digital moment to define their culture to candidates. Working with Airbnb executives and HR stakeholders at Symphony Studio, I established their Employer Brand Proposition — the values and benefits that differentiate Airbnb from not only their competitors, but the workforce at large. The result: responsive, mobile-friendly content articulating their Live and Work Anywhere policy that speaks simply, directly, and strategically to candidates.
impact: 72% increase in applications first post-launch quarter
170% increase in applications from underrepresented groups
3.4x increase in page views vs. previous site
UX copy and IA reduced bounce rate on key job family pages
Modular content system supported 50+ job families with pre-approved, brand-consistent copy blocks

projects.title: Google GPay instrumentation
projects.campaignListDescription: GPay is a one-touch checkout experience that works seamlessly between applications and e-commerce sites. Like all things Google, it was designed to be efficient — instrumented with only a few lines of code. Which means video content for its I/O premiere needed to act likewise. So instead of a smug, stemwinding video that wastes a developer's time with phony rapport, we got out of our own way and told the simplest, most direct path to instrumentation.
impact: 35K views during I/O launch event
1.5M ARR impact
~500 new integrations from live merchants

projects.title: Sentry developer content
projects.campaignListDescription: Sentry is an application monitoring platform that optimizes code health — tracking metrics and logs so CI/CD teams can resolve issues, optimize performance, and improve user experience. Because Sentry is a product built by developers for developers, content needed to speak their language (whether that's Rust, React, or Python) while also engaging CI/CD leaders on the platform's larger business value. This meant creating content that identified with a developer's daily struggles — not talking down with oversimplified language, nor wallowing in technical lingo that flies over their heads, but writing at eye level. Worked across San Francisco and Vienna, Austria engineering teams.
impact: ARR: $45M → $90M during my tenure (Series B→C growth), contributing to $1B unicorn valuation
~480 SQL conversions (9.5% attributed to ARR)
~150K page visits in total
Grew developer social following from 15K to 84K through technically accurate, developer-native content
Cross-timezone content workflow with US + Vienna (Austria) engineering teams

Built GitHub-based editorial workflow cutting production time from 3–4 weeks to 1–2 weeks; templates reduced start-from-scratch time by 40%

projects.title: Ceros | Fintech infographics
projects.campaignListDescription: Most digital content is still being written as if from a typewriter — despite being read on a screen. Static content written from this paper perspective, even if well-written and strategic, is viewed by customers as merely lumps of words. At Ceros, clients in trial often suffered from blank platform syndrome: an appreciation for the tool itself but a lack of understanding of its application to their broader content initiatives. From writing strategic briefs to pitching experiential activations and rewriting client inputs, I was central to turning static PDFs into interactive, mobile-friendly financial content that showcased the platform.
impact: 3x time spent on site compared to PDF content
1.75x SQL leads per month generated
Ceros closed financial clients producing this content while in trial

projects.title: Chronosphere | Observability content
projects.campaignListDescription: Technical thought leadership content for Chronosphere's cloud-native observability platform — translating complex monitoring concepts for engineering leaders evaluating modern observability stacks.

projects.title: Text | UX & content audit
projects.campaignListDescription: Users give a website 50 milliseconds to decide whether to stay or bounce. I audited Text's homepage and Hesitant Buyer landing page to identify where messaging was losing that bet — and where it could win it back. Key findings: above-the-fold copy underselling a differentiated value prop; nine CTAs all routing to the same trial signup (no content offramps, no trust-building middle step); and a 'Hesitant Buyer' page whose headline promised awareness-stage content but delivered middle-funnel product specs. Recommendations focused on value prop clarity, CTA balance, funnel-stage alignment, and substantiating big claims with data.
